nits: Tentative lease residue after pause: pause UPDATE leaves next_poll_at at the 5-min lease value rather than resetting it. Harmless today (sweep filters paused_at IS NULL), but if an operator unpauses by clearing paused_at the row will skip its due window by up to 5 minutes. Consider `next_poll_at = now()` in the pause UPDATE.
TENTATIVE_LEASE_SECONDS (300s) is longer than typical Lambda execution time; document the relationship near the const so future operators don't accidentally invert it.

Test #2's smart-mock serializes SELECTs via JS event-loop ordering rather than truly modelling DB row-level lock contention; the unit test proves call shape but not the DB invariant — the spec already calls for live e2e self-invocation verification.
The H.1 spec block doesn't mention the POLL_TENTATIVE_LEASE_SECONDS env override; worth adding for grep-discoverability.

`due` returned from sql.begin() is implicitly typed via the inner tx<Array<DueRow>> generic; an explicit annotation on the outer `const due: Array<DueRow>` would harden against future refactors widening it to unknown.
reviewer: opus-4.7 adversarial pass via Agent tool
